nodes.role: AST-level language discovery utility that identifies, tracks, and resolves programming languages within the codebase. Provides snapshot and delta computation for language landscape tracking.
responsibilities: Scans a list of file paths and extracts unique file extensions to build a LanguageSnapshot.
Computes the difference (LanguageDelta) between two language snapshots, identifying new, removed, and status-changed extensions.
Resolves a file's programming language by delegating to LangRegistry based on its file extension.
Provides sorted output of detected extensions for consistent representation.
Maintain a registry of LangConfig entries that associate source language identifiers (SourceLanguage) with corresponding @ast-grep/napi Lang objects and optional dynamic loaders.
Provide lookup methods for retrieving the correct Lang object given a file path or SourceLanguage, enabling AST extraction for scanning and analysis.
Manage dynamic language registration (e.g., for plugins) by ensuring idempotent calls to registerDynamicLanguage and caching the promise to avoid concurrent re-registration.
Expose configuration interfaces (LangConfig, InternalLangConfig) for defining language support in the system's language rules and scanner configuration.
Parse source code files for multiple programming languages using `@ast-grep/napi` and language-specific AST patterns defined in YAML rule files.
Extract exported symbols (classes, functions, variables, interfaces, types) from source files and return structured `ExtractedSymbol` objects.
Extract import statements, including source module paths and imported symbols, returning `ExtractedImport` objects.
Load and cache language-specific AST rules from YAML files, using `LangRegistry` to resolve supported languages and announce unsupported ones.
Produce a `FileSkeleton` structure summarizing a file's imports and exports for downstream processing.
Specifies how to detect and extract import statements (#include directives).
Defines export patterns for functions, classes, structs, namespaces, and template classes using tree-sitter AST queries.
Defines usage patterns for function calls, scoped access (::), and method calls (.).
Maps each extracted symbol to a semantic kind (Function, Class, Type).
Specifies how to detect import statements (import, alias, require, use) in Elixir source code.
Defines export pattern rules for functions, private functions, macros, modules, protocols, and implementations.
Provides usage pattern rules for function calls and module-qualified function calls.
Serves as a language-specific rule set for the ArchSpine code analysis and governance engine.
Specifies import pattern recognition for Go single and grouped imports.
Specifies export pattern recognition for Go functions, structs, interfaces, type aliases, variables, and constants.
Specifies usage pattern recognition for Go function calls and field/method selectors.
Specifies pattern-based rules for identifying Java import statements.
Defines how to recognize Java class and interface definitions (including public variants).
Describes usage detection patterns for method calls and object instantiation.
Provides the foundation for automatic extraction of structural metadata from Java source files.
Specifies patterns for importing modules
Defines export patterns for declarations: functions (including private and suspend), classes (standard, data, sealed), interfaces, objects, companion objects, variables (val/var), and typealiases
Provides usage patterns for function calls and property accesses
Serves as a Kotlin-specific grammar rule file for the ArchSpine scanner engine
Define import detection patterns (use, require, require_once, include, include_once)
Define export detection patterns (namespace, class, function)
Define usage detection patterns (call, method, static)
Support ArchSpine's AST extraction and dependency analysis for PHP projects
Specifies pattern templates for matching import statements (from-import and simple import).
Specifies pattern templates for matching export declarations (__all__, functions, classes, async functions, variables).
Specifies pattern templates for matching usage occurrences (function calls and property access).
Acts as a language-specific rule file that the ArchSpine AST extractor uses to build a symbol map of a Python codebase.
Repository scanning and file indexing
Rule-based architecture enforcement (layered architecture, naming conventions)
AST extraction for multiple programming languages
Synchronization between source repository and mirrored artifacts
View generation (architecture diagrams, public surface, risk hotspots, project health)
Integration with LLM providers and MCP tools
Manifest management and integrity verification
AI agent briefing and change impact analysis
Sync, check, fix, scan, view, init, try, remove, config, info, rules, skill CLI commands
Documentation and atlas for multi-language support
Specifies how Rust import statements are matched using the `use $SOURCE` pattern.
Defines export extractions for Rust functions (including parameters), structs, traits, implementations, and mutable/immutable variables.
Outlines usage pattern detection for function calls and path-based member accesses (e.g., `$OBJ::$NAME`).
Serves as a pluggable rule document that the ArchSpine scanner loads to perform language-specific static analysis.
Maintains compatibility with the ArchSpine protocol schemas for rules and manifest.
Define patterns for identifying Scala imports, exports (functions, classes, objects, traits, etc.), and usages (calls, property access) in source code.
Provide the mapping of Scala language constructs to abstract syntax tree (AST) node kinds for analysis and indexing.
Provides import detection patterns (e.g., 'import $SOURCE') to map Swift module dependencies.
Defines export/declaration patterns for Swift constructs: classes, structs, enums, protocols, and functions, with appropriate kind classification (Class, Interface, Function).
Specifies usage patterns for function calls and method invocations to trace how symbols are used across the codebase.
Serves as a machine-readable rule file that the ArchSpine AST extractor uses to parse Swift source files during a scan.
Configures import pattern matching (named, default, namespace)
Configures export pattern matching (function, class, interface, type, variable, default, list)
Configures usage pattern matching (call, new, property access)
Maintenance scope: updated when TypeScript syntax evolves or new patterns are needed
invariants: Each file extension maps to exactly one language via LangRegistry resolution.
The detected extensions in a snapshot are always sorted alphabetically.
For a given SourceLanguage, getLanguage() always returns the same Lang instance within a process lifetime.
Dynamic language registration is performed at most once; subsequent calls return the cached promise without re-registering.
All registered LangConfigs have a unique langKey and sourceLanguage to avoid conflicts.
Every call to `ASTExtractor` methods must have the language-specific YAML rules available in the expected directory structure.
Language support is determined by the presence of a corresponding YAML file in the rules directory, registered via `LangRegistry`.
The extraction relies on `@ast-grep/napi` node patterns; malformed or unexpected syntax may produce incomplete results.

publicSurface.description: Scans an array of file paths, extracts unique file extensions, and returns a LanguageSnapshot containing the sorted list of detected extensions.
Compares two LanguageSnapshots and returns a LanguageDelta listing new, removed, and status-changed extensions between them.
Resolves the programming language for a given file path by delegating to LangRegistry, returning the language name and support level.

Singleton-style class that manages the mapping from SourceLanguage to @ast-grep/napi Lang objects. Provides static methods for language resolution and dynamic registration. Consumed by the scanner, AST extractor, and any component that needs to obtain a Lang instance for a given source file.

Interface defining the configuration for a single language entry: ruleName (written name), langKey (the Lang or string identifier), sourceLanguage (canonical SourceLanguage enum), and an optional loader function for dynamic imports. Used when populating the registry with built-in or plugin languages.

Extends LangConfig with an optional packageName field used internally to track the npm package that provides dynamic language support. Exported for type consistency across the language registration system.
Class that parses source files and extracts symbols and imports using ast-grep and language-specific YAML rules. The primary entry point for all AST extraction operations in the ArchSpine pipeline.
Interface representing a single extracted symbol: its name, kind (Class, Function, Variable, Interface, Type, Unknown), signature, and optional implementation clue.
Interface representing an import statement: the source module path and the list of imported symbol names.
Interface describing the overall structure of a parsed file, including its imports and exported symbols.

nodes.role: Configuration schema types for the ArchSpine project — defines the structure of the `spine.config.json` file and related type aliases.
responsibilities: Define the `SpineConfig` interface that describes the project configuration schema, including project name, locales, and LLM settings.
Define the `MCPContextMode` type alias for the context mode used by MCP tools.
Import and re-export the `SchemaVersion` type from the versions module to enforce versioning on configuration files.
Import the `PartialScanPolicy` type from scan-policy to allow embedding partial scan policies within the configuration.
Define the SpineUnit interface, the primary index entry shape for file-level metadata in the atlas.
Define HealthFlags to attach per-file graph diagnostics (fan-in, hub status, cycle membership).
Define SpineIdentity, SpineSemantic, SpineSkeleton, ArchSpine, and SpineProvenance interfaces that together describe a file's identity, meaning, structure, dependency graph, and origin.
Define RuleViolation, Invariant, ChangeIntent, and PublicSurfaceEntry interfaces for governance and change tracking.
Define folder-level and project-level unit interfaces (SpineFolderUnit, FolderChild, SpineProjectUnit, ProjectModule) for hierarchical organization.
Define string-literal type aliases (SourceLanguage, FileKind, SymbolKind, DependencyRelation, EdgeProvenance, SymbolRelation, PipelineStage) to constrain the protocol domain.
Acts as the single import entry point for all protocol-level types (config, documents, languages, manifest, rules, versions).
Encapsulates the internal module structure of the protocol types directory, providing a stable public API surface.
Preserves compatibility by re-exporting individual modules without adding or modifying symbols.
Define the shape of a language detection snapshot (LanguageSnapshot), including schema version, detected file extensions, and per-language support status.
Define the LanguageSupport interface describing the support status (available, unavailable, unsupported) along with optional reason.
Define the LanguageDelta interface representing changes between two language snapshots.
Define the SyncBlock interface for tracking synchronization state
Define the DocRef interface for referencing documents by locale and path
Define the FileStatus interface for tracking file-level status in the manifest
Define the SpineManifest interface as the root schema for the entire Spine project manifest
Define the `SpineRuleDocument` interface as the schema-contract for all rule documents consumed and produced by the ArchSpine pipeline.
Import the shared `SchemaVersion` type to ensure version consistency across the protocol type hierarchy.
Defines the SchemaVersion type as a semantic versioning string template literal.
Exports the current package version string ('1.0.0') for external use.
Exports the current schema version constant used across the protocol.
Exports the current config schema version constant for configuration validation.
Exports a generator version string combining package name and version.
Provides a type guard function to verify if an unknown value equals the current config schema version.
Provide a unified import surface for all protocol type definitions by re-exporting from src/types/protocol/index.js.
Abstract the internal domain split of protocol types, enabling callers to import from a single path.
Prevent importing from internal protocol submodules directly, enforcing the module boundary.
Define ViewId and ViewType union types enumerating all supported view identifiers.
Define PublicSurfaceKind and ArchNodeType union types for categorization of view items.
Define IViewScoreContribution interface for scoring factors in view quality assessment.
Define IViewQualityMetadata interface for aggregate quality metadata on views.
Define IPublicSurfaceViewItem interface representing a single public surface entry in the view.
Define IRiskHotspotViewItem interface representing a risk hotspot entry.
Define IArchDiagramNode, IArchDiagramEdge, IArchDiagramSummaryCard, IArchDiagramSpec interfaces for architecture diagram data.
Re-export the SchemaVersion type from the protocol module for use in view type definitions.
invariants: Configuration schema must include a `schemaVersion` field of type `SchemaVersion`.
Configuration must include a `project` object with `name` and `locales` fields.
Configuration may optionally include an `llm` object with `provider`, `model`, and `baseURL` fields.
All exported interfaces must be part of the public protocol and must not be prefixed with 'I' (they are public types, not internal).
Type aliases must use string literal unions to enforce domain boundaries.
Interfaces must remain read-only (all fields using `readonly`) to ensure immutability constraints.
All exports from the re-exported submodules are part of this module's contract; any change in those submodules changes the public surface.
This module must not define new types or functions locally; it must remain a pure barrel file.
Interface names in the re-exported modules must conform to the 'I' prefix rule (enforced by rule interface-prefix).
Interfaces in this file are part of the public protocol and must not be prefixed with 'I' (the internal prefix rule applies only to non-exported interfaces).
All manifest data must conform to the defined TypeScript interfaces
SyncBlock.lastSyncMode must be one of 'full', 'incremental', or 'unknown'
DocRef must always include a locale string and a path string
All internal interfaces must start with the character 'I' (rule `interface-prefix`).
SchemaVersion must always be a string in the format `<major>.<minor>.<patch>`.
The config schema version is fixed to CURRENT_CONFIG_SCHEMA_VERSION; only exact matches are considered supported.
The rule 'interface-prefix' is not applicable as no interfaces are defined in this file.
The file must always re-export all symbols from './protocol/index.js' (via `export *`).
No additional exports or imports should be added to this file; it must remain a pure re-export facade.
The file path must remain as src/types/protocol.ts to maintain the public API contract.
All view interfaces must be prefixed with 'I' to comply with the interface-prefix naming convention.
ViewId and ViewType are equivalent string unions representing the same set of identifiers.
PublicSurfaceKind and ArchNodeType are string literal unions, not enums.
IArchDiagramSpec uses generic type NodeMeta for flexibility in node metadata.

publicSurface.description: The primary configuration interface for the ArchSpine project. Consumed by configuration loaders and validators to enforce the shape of `spine.config.json`.
A type alias defining the available context modes for MCP operations. Used by MCP server and tool configurations.
The top-level index entry shape for a single file, combining identity, semantic summary, structural skeleton, dependency graph, provenance, and optional health flags.
Graph-health diagnostics attached to each index entry by KnowledgeGraphTask, recording fan-in, hub status, and cycle membership.
Identifies a file or directory by path, hash, and language classification.
Captures the architectural role, responsibilities, and invariants extracted by summarization.
Describes a single architectural rule violation detected during checks, including rule ID, severity, and reason.
Represents an invariant that must hold for the module, derived from architectural rules.
Documents the architectural and recent change intent for a file, used for drift detection.
An entry in a module's public API surface, describing a single exported symbol's contract.
Structural skeleton of a source file, enumerating imports, exports, and declared symbols.
Represents a single import statement within a file's skeleton.
Represents a single export statement within a file's skeleton.
Represents a symbol declared in a file, with name, kind, and signature.
Optional metadata hints about a file's structure, such as language or pattern type.
Dependency graph representation for a file, listing file-level and symbol-level edges with relation types.
A directed dependency edge between two files, tracking relation type and provenance.
A directed dependency edge between two symbols, tracking the relation and providing symbolic granularity.
Metadata about how a SpineUnit was created, including tool version, task, and timestamp.
Index entry for a directory/folder, listing child files and subdirectories with their identity and health flags.
Describes a child entry (file or directory) within a folder unit, with path and health flags.
Top-level project unit containing configuration summary, module references, and root folder unit reference.
Describes a module within a project unit, including its name, path, and language.
String-literal type union enumerating all supported source languages (e.g., 'typescript', 'python').
String-literal type union classifying file kinds (e.g., 'source', 'test', 'config').
String-literal type union for declared symbol kinds (e.g., 'function', 'class', 'interface').
String-literal type union for dependency relationship types (e.g., 'imports', 'calls', 'extends').
String-literal type union describing how an edge was discovered (e.g., 'ast', 'inference').
String-literal type union for symbol-level relationship types (e.g., 'imports', 'calls', 'implements').
String-literal type union for pipeline stage identifiers (e.g., 'scan', 'summarize', 'aggregate').
Re-exports all symbols from './config.js', providing configuration-related type definitions (e.g., project config schema, environment settings).
Re-exports all symbols from './index-documents.js', providing type definitions for indexed document structures used in search and retrieval.
Re-exports all symbols from './languages.js', providing type definitions for supported programming languages and language detection.
Re-exports all symbols from './manifest.js', providing type definitions for the ArchSpine manifest format.
Re-exports all symbols from './rules.js', providing type definitions for architectural rules and rule enforcement.
Re-exports all symbols from './versions.js', providing type definitions for protocol versioning and compatibility.
Container for the complete state of language detection: schema version, detected extensions, and a map of language support records.
Describes the detection status of a single language (available, unavailable, unsupported) with an optional explanation string.
Represents the difference between two language snapshots, used to propagate changes in language support.
Interface describing the current synchronization state of the manifest, including the last sync timestamp, mode, reverse index completion flag, and count of indexed units.
Interface representing a reference to a localized document, containing a locale identifier and a file path.
Interface for tracking the status of a single file within the manifest, such as whether it is indexed, dirty, or unchanged.
Top-level interface for the Spine project manifest, aggregating all metadata, languages, rules, and sync state into a single schema object.

Interface defining the canonical schema for a rule document in the ArchSpine protocol. Contains fields for rule ID, title, summary, applicability criteria, severity, enforceability flag, rationale, and the full body content. Used by rules loaders, validation engines, and view renderers.
Type alias for a semantic version string template literal. Used throughout the codebase to type-check schema versions.
The current release version of the ArchSpine package. Used for display and build metadata.
The current schema version of the ArchSpine manifest and protocol. Used to validate serialized data compatibility.
The current config schema version for .spine/config.json files. Used to determine if a config file is supported.
A generator identifier string in the form 'archspine/<version>'. Used in generated output to mark its origin.
Type guard that checks whether an unknown value equals the current config schema version. Returns true only for exact matches.
Re-exports all type definitions from src/types/protocol/index.js. External modules import from this file to access all protocol-related types without depending on internal module structure.
Union of all supported view identifiers: 'public-surface', 'risk-hotspots', 'architecture-diagram', 'project-health', 'agent-briefing', 'change-impact'.
Alias for ViewId; represents the type of a view.
Union of string literals categorizing public surface items (e.g., 'function', 'class', 'type', 'interface').
Union of string literals categorizing architecture diagram nodes (e.g., 'module', 'layer', 'component').
Interface representing a single scoring factor contribution with a factor name, score, and reason.
Interface for metadata about a view's quality, including an overall score, version, and list of score contributions.
Interface representing a single public surface item with name, kind, file path, etc.
Interface representing a risk hotspot with file path, risk category, severity, and description.
Interface representing a node in an architecture diagram with id, label, type, and optional metadata.
Interface representing a directed edge between two architecture diagram nodes.
Interface for a summary card in an architecture diagram view, containing a title, value, and optional trend.
Generic interface representing the full architecture diagram specification including nodes, edges, and summary cards with typed node metadata.

nodes.role: CLI utility module providing formatting, error handling, banner display, and help text wrapping for the ArchSpine command-line interface.
responsibilities: Parse raw string configuration values into typed equivalents (boolean, number, JSON object) for CLI argument handling.
Format typed configuration values back into display strings for user feedback.
Throw standardized CLI usage errors with exit code 1 via ArchSpineError, ensuring consistent error behavior across commands.
Normalize optional string arguments by returning undefined for empty or whitespace-only inputs.
Print step messages to stdout with optional inline TTY mode for live progress feedback.
Print a formatted language discovery summary table showing detected languages and their availability counts.
Display an appropriate UI banner (full or mini) based on the command context, suppressing banners during internal hooks to avoid log pollution.
Wrap long prompt text to a configurable line width with optional indentation for readable console output.
Parse and validate CLI arguments for the build command and display a UI banner.
Delegate to runBuildWorkflow and runSyncWorkflow to execute build and sync steps.
Export a reusable runBuildWorkflow function for invocation by other modules (e.g., try, sync) without CLI coupling.
Prints an experimental warning banner before running the check to inform users about the feature's maturity.
Delegates the actual architectural rule validation to `RuntimeService.getCheckService().run()`, keeping the CLI layer thin.
Catches `ArchSpineError` with code `CliCommandFailed` and surfaces the error to the user.
Defines `ExecuteCheckCommandOptions` interface to inject the required RuntimeService dependency.
Parse CLI arguments and dispatch subcommands (get, set, list, languages, repo, hooks) for configuration management.
Interact with Config and Manifest objects to read and persist configuration state, including language settings and repository artifact strategies.
Manage documentation language selection by prompting users interactively or resolving from command arguments.
Install and uninstall Git hooks based on user configuration preferences.
Validate configuration keys and values before applying changes.
Render configuration output to the console in a human-readable format.
Execute the `info` CLI command by parsing arguments and delegating to `runInfoReport` or `runUsageReport` based on flags.
Retrieve and display project sync status from the runtime service (`runtimeService.getSyncStatusService().status`).
Translate errors into user-facing messages using `toArchSpineError` from the core error system.
Pass `defaultRuntimeIO` as the I/O abstraction to engine functions for consistent output.
Parse CLI arguments specific to the init command (--agent-file, --artifact-strategy, --inject-package-scripts).
Determine artifact strategy from command-line argument or fall back to default.
Configure initial documentation languages by prompting user if not persisted.
Bootstrap the repository structure via runRepositoryBootstrap.
Bootstrap the runtime database (SQLite) via runRuntimeBootstrap.
Output success confirmation after completion.
Parse CLI arguments to determine the LLM scope (global vs project) and extract command-specific arguments.
Resolve the correct config/secrets store for a given scope (global or project).
Present interactive prompts to configure LLM provider, model, base URL, and API key.
Display current LLM configuration status, including provider, model, base URL, and API key presence.
Offer a predefined list of provider choices (OpenAI, etc.) and map selections to configuration presets.
Execute the full LLM configuration command: handle 'set', 'clear', 'status' subcommands or trigger interactive setup.
Start an MCP server by instantiating ArchSpineMCPServer and calling start()
Configure MCP client config files by invoking setupMcpConfigs and presenting results to the user
Route MCP subcommands based on CLI arguments, calling the appropriate handler with validated options
Orchestrates the removal of ArchSpine-managed files from a repository (agent instructions, .gitignore, .gitattributes, .spineignore, .searchignore).
Uninstalls the pre-commit git hook installed by ArchSpine.
Removes ArchSpine-injected scripts from package.json.
Prompts for user confirmation before performing destructive removal (unless --yes flag is provided).
Parses the subcommand from command-line arguments to determine the rule operation (list, add, info).
Delegates to `listRuleTemplates`, `addRuleTemplate`, and `getRuleTemplateInfo` from the rule service for actual business logic.
Handles CLI output by printing results or error messages to the console.
Calls `throwCliUsage` to display usage information when an invalid subcommand is provided.
Parse and validate CLI arguments for the scan command, including flags like --quick, --dry-run, and positional patterns.
Initialize a Scanner engine instance using the provided configuration's scan policy.
Execute a full scan via Scanner or a quick scan via runQuickScan depending on the --quick flag.
Persist quick scan results to disk using saveQuickScanResults when applicable.
Print a dry-run summary report generated by Scanner if --dry-run is specified.
Display scanned file count, language statistics, and dependency graph node/edge counts after a full scan.
Throw usage errors for unrecognized flags or invalid argument combinations.
Parse and validate CLI arguments for the sync command (e.g., --full, --hook, --repair, --retry-failed, --surface).
Display UI banner and route to appropriate sync mode (full scan, incremental hook, repair).
Orchestrate the full sync workflow: load execution checkpoint, run scanner, invoke LLM analysis via RuntimeService, update manifest, apply repair policy, and detect protected output mutations.
Handle errors by classifying with ArchSpineError and providing user-facing error messages.
Define the interface ExecuteSyncCommandOptions to type the command parameters.
Parse and validate CLI arguments for the 'try' command, providing usage instructions on failure.
Delegate to TryService.checkRepoPosture to validate the repository state.
Delegate to TryService.discoverLanguageLandscape to detect programming languages used in the project.
Delegate to TryService.generateASTPreviews to produce AST samples for detected languages.
Format and print the combined result (posture, languages, AST previews) to the console via console.log.
Accept optional banner callback for consistent UI display across commands.
Parse CLI subcommands ('show', 'list', 'info', 'configure') from the 'arch-spine view' command and dispatch to appropriate handlers.
Interactively prompt users to select which views to enable when configuring view settings.
Resolve and normalize enabled view IDs and layer configurations by delegating to the view service layer.
Orchestrate writing baseline files for protected outputs by directly invoking writeProtectedOutputBaseline and withProtectedOutputsWriteAccess.
Display current view configuration and enabled view information to the user.
Print the ArchSpine product dashboard (sync status, enabled views, active rules) by delegating to DashboardService.
Print general CLI usage help text listing all available commands.
Print detailed help text for a specific CLI command based on command name.
Configure global HTTP/HTTPS proxy agent from environment variables at startup using the 'undici' library.
Parse CLI arguments and display banners, general help, or command-specific help as appropriate.
Dispatch top-level commands (init, sync, build, llm, remove, check, scan, config, mcp, info, view, try, skill, rules) to their respective execute*Command functions.
Provide usage error messages and early exit for unrecognized commands or missing arguments.
Load configuration via Config, Manifest, Secrets, and LLM configuration to determine if a project or baseline exists, influencing the command flow.
Determines the artifact strategy (e.g., git-based tracking) by querying configuration based on init options.
Prompts the user to confirm installation of recommended rule templates and delegates installation to the repository admin service.
Offers to install a pre-commit git hook, manages user consent, and updates configuration accordingly.
Collects and validates the user's name and email for agent instructions attribution.
Synchronizes agent instructions configuration by calling the repository admin service.
Manages the overall interactive bootstrap sequence, providing progress feedback via console output.
Orchestrate the full bootstrap sequence for a new project: resolve LLM settings, prompt for LLM setup if not configured, discover project languages, and optionally run the initial build.
Prompt the user to confirm initial synchronization (sync) before proceeding with the build.
Display language discovery results and step-by-step progress via console output.
Parse and normalize a string value to a valid ArtifactStrategy ('local' or 'distributable') or return undefined for invalid input.
Check the current repository strategy by delegating to checkRepositoryStrategy and report the result via console output, raising an error if no strategy is detected.
Set the artifact strategy for a repository by delegating to applyRepositoryStrategy, orchestrating the process with step-by-step progress printing via printStep.
Parse the first CLI argument as a subcommand (install, uninstall, list, etc.).
Invoke `installSkill` from the skill service with appropriate arguments for the 'install' subcommand.
Invoke `uninstallSkill` from the skill service with appropriate arguments for the 'uninstall' subcommand.
Display a list of installed skills with their descriptions for the 'list' subcommand.
Display usage help and exit with error code when an unknown subcommand is provided.
Provide the typed interface `ExecuteSkillCommandOptions` for the command options contract.
Define the DocumentLanguageChoice interface for representing a selectable language option in a CLI prompt.
Export the sentinel value HIGH_CAPACITY_LANGUAGE_SEPARATOR_VALUE for use as a UI separator marker.
Export the visible separator label text HIGH_CAPACITY_LANGUAGE_SEPARATOR.

Must remain a thin CLI adapter, delegating business logic to service modules; must not absorb pipeline or persistence logic.
All user-facing output must be through console.log/console.warn or delegated printStep helper.
The CLI entrypoint must not contain business logic or persistence logic; this file adheres by delegating entirely to skill-service.
The subcommand must be the first element of the args array.
Must remain free of any persistence, pipeline, or LLM logic (per cli-entrypoint-separation rule).
Must only provide data structure definitions and a pure function to transform language lists into choice objects.
The exported types must remain backward-compatible with all init entrypoints (`init.ts`, `repository-bootstrap.ts`, `runtime-bootstrap.ts`).
`InitSharedOptions.rootDir`, `Config`, and `RuntimeService` must always be provided for bootstrap execution.

publicSurface.description: Parses a raw string CLI argument into a typed value (boolean, number, or JSON object) for downstream configuration use.
Formats a typed configuration value back into a display-friendly string for user-facing output.
Throws an ArchSpineError with CliUsageInvalid code and exit code 1, signaling invalid CLI usage to the user.
Normalizes an optional string argument by returning undefined if the value is empty or whitespace-only, preserving the original otherwise.
Prints a step message to stdout with an optional inline TTY mode that overwrites the previous line for live progress updates.
Prints a formatted table showing detected programming languages and their available/parseable counts from a LanguageSnapshot.
Displays the full or mini ArchSpine banner based on the command context, suppressing it during internal hooks to avoid log pollution.
Wraps long prompt text to a specified maximum line width with optional indentation for readable console output.
CLI handler for the build command; parses arguments and triggers the build workflow. Called by the CLI router after command dispatch.
Executes the build workflow (sync + build) programmatically, accepting rootDir, Config, and RuntimeService. Used by other CLI commands and services to run builds without CLI context.
Configuration interface for executeBuildCommand, defining CLI args, rootDir, config, runtimeService, and a displayUIBanner callback.
Orchestrates the `spine check` subcommand: parses flags (e.g. --fresh), delegates to the runtime service for validation, and presents results/errors to the console. Called by the CLI router.
Configuration interface for the check command, requiring an array of CLI arguments and an instance of RuntimeService. Injected by the CLI entrypoint to keep the handler decoupled.
Prompts the user via an interactive menu to select which languages to generate documentation for, returning the chosen language codes or undefined if cancelled.
Main entry point for the `spine config` CLI command; parses sub-arguments and dispatches configuration operations such as getting/setting keys, managing languages, repository settings, and git hooks.
Interface defining the expected arguments (`args`, `config`, `rootDir`, `runtimeService`) for the executeConfigCommand function.
Configuration interface for the info command execution, containing CLI arguments, root directory, and an optional RuntimeService instance.
Asynchronously executes the info command: displays sync status and, based on CLI flags, runs the detailed information report or usage report by delegating to engine modules.
Main exported function that executes the init command. Called by the CLI dispatcher to set up a new project repository and runtime.
Interface defining options for executeInitCommand, including args array, config provider, runtime service, and optional output manager.
Main entry point for the 'llm' CLI command. Parses subcommands and arguments, then either invokes interactive setup or executes a subcommand like status/set/clear via delegated services.
Parses raw CLI argument array to detect the --global / --project flag and returns the scope along with the remaining command-specific arguments.
Returns the appropriate Config and Secrets instances for the given scope (global or project), enabling targeted LLM configuration writes.
Runs an interactive prompt sequence (provider, model, base URL, API key) and writes the results to the provided Config and Secrets stores.
Fetches the resolved LLM status from RuntimeService and prints a human-readable summary of the current provider, model, base URL, and API key state.
Returns an array of provider option objects suitable for the 'prompts' library (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini) used during LLM setup.
Maps a provider selection string to a preset object containing the provider name and an optional default model name.
Interface describing the options object passed to executeLlmCommand, including args, rootDir, config, secrets, global config, global secrets, and runtime service.
Type alias for a subset of Config interface that only exposes LLM writing methods (setLLMProvider, setLLMModel, setLLMBaseURL).
Type alias for a subset of Secrets interface that only exposes API key writing and clearing methods (setLLMApiKey, clearLLMApiKey).
Union type of 'global' | 'project' used to distinguish between user-level and project-level LLM configuration.
Interface that defines the options object for the MCP command handler: args, rootDir, and config.
Starts an MCP server for the given root directory by creating an ArchSpineMCPServer instance and calling its start method.
Configures MCP client settings for the project by calling setupMcpConfigs and logging status to the user.
Dispatches the MCP subcommand (start, setup) based on the provided args array, or throws usage on invalid arguments.
Configuration interface for the remove command, specifying CLI arguments, root directory, and config instance.
Main entrypoint that uninstalls all ArchSpine-managed artifacts from a repository. Called by the CLI dispatcher when the 'remove' subcommand is invoked.
Entry point for the 'rules' CLI command. Parses the subcommand from args and delegates to rule service functions. Called by the CLI dispatcher.
Interface defining the options for executeRulesCommand: an array of args and an optional root directory.

Async function that executes the scan CLI command: parses arguments, triggers full or quick scanning via engine modules, saves quick scan results, prints a dry-run report if requested, and reports file/language/dependency statistics to the console.
Interface for the options object consumed by executeScanCommand, containing parsed CLI args, the project root directory, and a Config instance.
Interface defining the parameters required to invoke the sync command, including CLI args, root directory, config, runtime service, UI banner display function, and optional LLM runtime overrides.
Primary entry point for the sync CLI command. Parses CLI arguments, validates flags, displays UI banner, and dispatches to `runSyncWorkflow` with the appropriate parameters.

Orchestrates the full synchronization pipeline: loads manifest and execution checkpoint, invokes Scanner for file discovery, runs LLM analysis via RuntimeService, updates manifest, applies repair policy, and detects protected output mutations. Accepts options for mode (full/hook), repair flag, retry-failed, origin, and surface type.
Options interface for the try command handler, providing CLI arguments, project root directory, and an optional banner display callback. Used by the CLI framework to invoke the command.
Main entry point for the 'try' CLI command. It validates arguments, calls TryService to check repo posture, discover languages, and generate AST previews, then prints results to stdout.
Asynchronously prompts the user via interactive CLI to select which views to enable from the current set, returning the chosen view IDs or undefined if cancelled.

Main entrypoint for the 'arch-spine view' CLI command, parsing the subcommand from args and dispatching to show, list, info, or configure logic, including syncing view configuration and writing protected output baselines.
Interface defining the configuration passed to executeViewCommand: CLI args, global config, runtime service, and LLM client.
Prints the ArchSpine product dashboard, including sync status, enabled views, and number of active rules, by fetching data from the DashboardService.
Prints general CLI usage help text listing all available commands.
Prints detailed help text for a specific CLI command identified by name (e.g., 'init', 'scan').

Orchestrates the interactive initialization of a repository's ArchSpine configuration. Called by the CLI init command to guide the user through setting up rules, pre-commit hooks, agent instructions, and artifact strategy.
Orchestrates the full runtime initialization sequence: checks LLM settings, prompts for setup, discovers languages, and optionally runs the build workflow. Called by the `spine init` CLI command.
Interactive confirmation prompt asking the user to agree to an initial file synchronization before the bootstrap proceeds.
Parses a raw string value and returns an ArtifactStrategy ('local' or 'distributable') if valid, otherwise undefined. Used by CLI commands to interpret user input for artifact strategy.

Checks the current repository artifact strategy using checkRepositoryStrategy and prints the result to the console. Raises an ArchSpineError if no strategy is detected. Called by the repo check CLI command.
Sets the artifact strategy for a repository by delegating to applyRepositoryStrategy, with step-by-step progress output via printStep. Called by the repo set CLI command.
Top-level CLI handler for the 'skill' command. Parses subcommands and delegates to the skill service layer. Called by the CLI router when the user runs `spine skill <subcommand>`.
Options interface specifying the command-line arguments (array of strings) to be passed to executeSkillCommand.
Interface for a language option item used in CLI prompts, providing title, value, and optional selection/disabled state.
Sentinel string value representing a UI separator for high-capacity languages in the language choice list.
Human-readable label text for the separator line displayed between standard and high-capacity language groups.
Constant note (currently empty) that can be used to display a quality or capability advisory alongside the language list.
Pure function that builds a full array of DocumentLanguageChoice objects from default and high-capacity language lists, marking selected items based on the input array of selected languages.
Interface that aggregates `rootDir`, `Config`, and `RuntimeService` instances required by init bootstrap modules. Consumed by `repository-bootstrap` and `runtime-bootstrap`.
Interface describing the result of repository bootstrapping, including flags for files created or updated. Consumed by init command handlers.
String literal type `'global' | 'project'` indicating the scope for LLM configuration. Used to constrain valid values in init prompts.
String literal type `'enabled' | 'disabled' | 'skipped-no-git-root'` representing the outcome of git hook setup attempts. Used to report status to the user.
String literal type `'local' | 'distributable'` defining how artifacts should be stored. Influences file placement during initialization.

nodes.role: Configuration validation layer in the infrastructure module, responsible for validating `SpineConfig` structures against the protocol schema before they are consumed by the rest of the system.
responsibilities: Validate an arbitrary value against the `SpineConfig` protocol schema, enumerating all validation issues.
Provide a typed result interface (`SpineConfigValidationResult`) for consumers to check success/failure.
Expose both a semantic resolver (`resolveSpineConfig`) and a convenience validator (`validateSpineConfig`).
Factory creation of initial TaskArtifactsState, TaskTelemetryState, and composite TaskState objects
Resetting pipeline state within a TaskContext with optional partial overrides
Tracking LLM usage metrics (token consumption, cost) into telemetry
Recording per-stage runtime metrics (duration, heap, RSS) for diagnostics and profiling
Maintaining monotonic counters for processed, skipped, and failed files
Caching file skeletons and unsupported-file markers in runtime-scoped artifacts
Queuing pending index commits (file hash, kind, and SpineUnit) for batch flush
Collecting drift warnings, summarize diagnostics, and validate diagnostics in telemetry
Providing lookup queries for cached skeletons and unsupported-file checks
Define TaskStats interface for tracking pipeline task counts and token usage.
Define TaskStageMetric interface for per-stage metrics.
Define TaskDiagnosticsState, TaskSelectionState, TaskArtifactsState, TaskTelemetryState to represent various aspects of task state.
Define TaskState as the aggregate container for all task state facets.
Define ScanStageInput and ScanStageOutput to contract the scan pipeline stage.
Define ExtractionStageOutput to contract the AST extraction stage.
Define FixViolation, FixStageInput, FixStageOutput to contract the fix pipeline stage.
Define CommitStageOutput to contract the commit stage.
Define ViewDerivationStageOutput to contract the view derivation stage.
Define the abstract SpineTask base class that all pipeline tasks must extend, establishing a uniform execution lifecycle.
Provide the TaskContext interface for sharing state and artifact metadata across tasks during a pipeline run.
Inject core engine dependencies (Scanner, Manifest, Aggregator, OutputManager, RuleEngine, ContextEngine, ASTExtractor) into task implementations.
Enforce type-safe input/output generics (<I, O>) for task contracts.
Define `ArchSpineError` as the base error class with code, cause, and context metadata for all system errors.
Declare `ArchSpineErrorCode` as the union type of all valid error codes.
Provide `ErrorCodes` constant mapping semantic error names to string codes (CLI, runtime, publish, config, etc.).
Expose `toArchSpineError` factory to wrap unknown errors into typed `ArchSpineError` instances with a fallback code and message.
Define `ArchSpineErrorOptions` interface for optional cause, context, and exitCode propagation.
Execute a `SpineTask` by calling `task.execute(input)` with the provided input.
Record stage start and completion via `ExecutionContextCheckpoint` for observability and recovery.
Record execution metrics (duration, memory usage) via `recordTaskStageMetric` after each stage.
Handle task execution failures by marking the stage as failed and logging the error via `RuntimeIO.error()`
Log informational messages at stage boundaries via `RuntimeIO.info()`
Define the FileSource type to select the set of files for scanning (git-tracked, git-tracked-plus-untracked, or full filesystem).
Define the ScanPolicy interface specifying file source, ignore chain (with inheritGitIgnore, projectIgnore, localIgnore), and protocol-level inclusion/exclusion lists.
Define the PartialScanPolicy interface allowing callers to override only specific fields of ScanPolicy.
Implement resolveScanPolicy to merge a partial policy with default values, producing a complete ScanPolicy.
Maintain protocol-level isolation: the policy definition must not depend on CLI or filesystem I/O.
invariants: Must not depend on CLI entry code (complies with core-pipeline-isolation rule).
Returned `SpineConfig` must strictly conform to the protocol version constants.
All task-state factory functions produce valid fully-initialized state objects with non-null collections
All state mutations operate exclusively through a TaskContext reference, never on detached state
File-path keys in runtime-cache Maps (skeletons, unsupportedFiles, pendingCommits) are always relative paths
Processed/skipped/failed counters are monotonically non-decreasing
Drift warnings and diagnostic snapshots are append-only collections
Core module must not import or depend on CLI entry code (core-pipeline-isolation rule)
This module only depends on other core and type protocol modules; it does not import any CLI entry code, satisfying the core-pipeline-isolation rule.
All pipeline tasks must extend SpineTask and implement its abstract execute method.
TaskContext must be consistently populated across all tasks in a single pipeline run.
Core modules (this file) must not depend on CLI entry code (src/cli/).
All modules in the ArchSpine system should use `ArchSpineError` as the canonical error type for propagation.
Error codes must be defined in the `ErrorCodes` constant and their union type `ArchSpineErrorCode` must remain consistent.
Core modules (like this one) must not import from CLI entry modules (satisfied: zero imports).
core-pipeline-isolation: The module must not depend on CLI entry code; it only imports from other core modules (task.js, task-state.js), which is compliant.
Must not import from CLI layer (core-pipeline-isolation rule).

publicSurface.description: Takes an unknown value and returns a `SpineConfigValidationResult` containing the parsed config (or null) and a list of validation issues. This is the primary entry point for config validation.
Convenience function that delegates to `resolveSpineConfig` and returns the same result. Intended for simple validation calls where the semantic distinction is not needed.
Interface defining the shape of the validation outcome: `config` (nullable SpineConfig) and `issues` (string array of validation errors/warnings).
Creates and returns a fresh TaskArtifactsState with empty skeleton, unsupported-files, and pending-commit maps. Called at the start of a pipeline run.
Creates and returns a fresh TaskTelemetryState initialized with the given diagnostics mode and zeroed counters. Called at the start of a pipeline run.
Creates and returns a complete TaskState by composing createTaskArtifactsState and createTaskTelemetryState with default values. Called when initializing a new TaskContext.
Resets the TaskContext state to a fresh baseline, preserving only the diagnostics mode and applying optional partial overrides. Called when re-initializing the pipeline for a new run.
Records LLM usage information (token counts, cost) into the task telemetry. Called by LLM-invoking stages after each model call.
Records a runtime metric snapshot (duration, heap, RSS) for a named pipeline stage. Called by each stage upon completion.
Increments the processed-file counter in telemetry stats. Called by stages after successfully handling a file.
Increments the skipped-file counter in telemetry stats. Called by stages when a file is intentionally skipped.
Increments the failed-file counter in telemetry stats. Called by stages when a file processing fails.
Marks a file path as unsupported with a human-readable reason in the runtime artifacts cache. Called by analyzers that cannot parse a file.
Checks whether a given file path has been previously marked as unsupported. Called by stages before attempting to process a file.
Stores a parsed FileSkeleton object in the runtime artifacts cache keyed by file path. Called by the AST extraction stage.
Retrieves a cached FileSkeleton by file path, or undefined if none exists. Called by stages that need file structure data.
Queues a pending commit record (hash, file kind, spine unit) for batch persistence. Called after a file is fully processed and needs indexing.
Appends a drift-warning entry containing file path and reason to the telemetry drift-warnings list. Called by the semantic-drift detection stage.
Pushes a diagnostic snapshot into the summarize diagnostics array within telemetry. Called by the summarization stage for observability.
Pushes a diagnostic snapshot into the validate diagnostics array within telemetry. Called by the validation stage for observability.
Tracks aggregate counts of processed, skipped, and failed tasks along with token usage across a pipeline run.
Captures metrics for a specific pipeline stage, such as duration or counts.
Holds diagnostic information for a task, including errors and warnings.
Records which tasks have been selected for processing or skipping.
Stores artifacts produced by a task, such as files or data structures.
Carries telemetry data associated with a task, e.g., duration or token counts.
Aggregate state combining diagnostics, selection, artifacts, and telemetry for a single task.
Input contract for the scan stage, specifying what to scan.
Output contract for the scan stage, containing scanned file information.
Output contract for the AST extraction stage, providing extracted symbols and structure.
Represents a single rule violation detected and targeted for automatic fixing.
Input contract for the fix stage, specifying violations to fix.
Output contract for the fix stage, detailing applied fixes and their results.
Output contract for the commit stage, indicating what was committed.
Output contract for the view derivation stage, providing derived view data.

Abstract base class for all pipeline tasks. Defines the execution contract with generic input/output types and receives core engine dependencies. All concrete pipeline tasks (e.g., scan, aggregate, validate) extend this class.
Interface providing shared state and artifacts coordination between tasks during a pipeline run. Used by SpineTask subclasses and the pipeline orchestrator.
Base error class for all ArchSpine system errors, carrying a machine-readable code, optional cause, and optional context metadata. Consumed by every layer for error propagation.
Interface for optional parameters when constructing an ArchSpineError, including cause, context, and exitCode.
Union type of all valid error code strings derived from the ErrorCodes constant. Used for type-safe error code references.
Constant dictionary mapping symbolic error names to string codes for CLI usage, runtime, publish, and configuration errors. Referenced by all error-throwing modules.
Converts an unknown error into a typed ArchSpineError with a provided fallback code and message. Used by catch clauses across the codebase to normalize error handling.
Core class that wraps a TaskContext and provides the `run` method to execute SpineTask instances with lifecycle instrumentation. Consumed by pipeline stages and orchestration services.
Accepts a TaskContext object, which provides runtime services (checkpoint, IO, metrics) that the runner uses during execution.

Asynchronously executes a SpineTask by calling its `execute` method with the provided input, managing checkpoint start/completion/failure and recording stage metrics. Returns the task's output type O.
Union type that selects the source of files for scanning: 'git-tracked', 'git-tracked-plus-untracked', or 'filesystem'. Used by scan engines to determine which files to process.
Complete interface specifying file source, ignore chain configuration, and protocol-level inclusion/exclusion arrays. Consumed by scanners and pipeline stages to control scan scope.
Partial version of ScanPolicy with all fields optional. Allows callers to override only specific policy fields without specifying the full policy.
Merges a PartialScanPolicy with default values to produce a complete ScanPolicy. Called by pipeline initialization and command handlers to compute the effective scan policy.

nodes.role: Pure utility module for computing deterministic content-addressed hashes (structural and semantic footprints) of file skeletons and semantic summaries.
responsibilities: Calculate a deterministic SHA-256 hash of a FileSkeleton, normalizing import and export ordering for stable comparison.
Calculate a deterministic SHA-256 hash of a SpineSemantic summary, normalizing public surface entries for stable comparison.
Normalize agent instruction filenames to ensure they match allowed patterns.
Synchronize managed gitignore entries into the project's .gitignore file.
Remove managed gitignore entries from the project's .gitignore file.
Synchronize managed gitattributes entries into the project's .gitattributes file.
Remove managed gitattributes entries from the project's .gitattributes file.
Synchronize the managed agent instructions file (e.g., CLAUDE.md) with ArchSpine blocks.
Remove the managed agent instructions file content from the host file.
Synchronize the managed search ignore file (e.g., .searchignore) with ArchSpine-managed lines.
Remove managed search ignore file content.
Synchronize the managed .spineignore file with ArchSpine-recommended lines.
Remove managed .spineignore file content.
Inject ArchSpine npm scripts (build, dev, etc.) into the project's package.json.
Remove ArchSpine npm scripts from package.json based on a tracked scripts list.
Synchronize the V2 briefing block content inside an agent instructions file.
Remove the V2 briefing block from an agent instructions file.
Synchronize the MCP tools block content inside an agent instructions file.
Remove the MCP tools block from an agent instructions file.
Synchronize the health block content inside an agent instructions file.
Remove the health block from an agent instructions file.
Define unique block start/end markers for embedding ArchSpine sections (v1 agent instructions, V2 knowledge graph briefing, MCP tools, health snapshot) into markdown documents.
Provide pre-assembled block strings (ARCHSPINE_AGENT_BLOCK, ARCHSPINE_V2_BRIEFING_BLOCK, ARCHSPINE_MCP_TOOLS_BLOCK, ARCHSPINE_HEALTH_BLOCK) for direct injection into agent instruction files.
Supply content arrays (ARCHSPINE_V2_BRIEFING_CONTENT, ARCHSPINE_MCP_TOOLS_CONTENT, ARCHSPINE_HEALTH_CONTENT) that form the inner text of those blocks.
Define file paths (SEARCH_IGNORE_PATH, SPINEIGNORE_PATH) for the .ignore and .spineignore files.
Provide recommended ignore lines for .spineignore (SPINEIGNORE_RECOMMENDED_LINES) and .gitignore (LOCAL_GITIGNORE_LINES), plus block start/end delimiters for managed sections.
Provide .gitignore and .gitattributes block start/end markers (GITIGNORE_BLOCK_START/END, GITATTRIBUTES_BLOCK_START/END) to encapsulate ArchSpine-managed regions.
Supply the full concatenated SEARCH_IGNORE_CONTENT for the .ignore file from the SEARCH_IGNORE_LINES list.
Re-export all exported symbols from './agent-instructions.templates.js' to provide template definitions for agent instructions.
Re-export all exported symbols from './agent-instructions.sync.js' to provide synchronization logic for agent instructions.
Reads the package version from package.json and caches it for reuse.
Renders a large, colorized ASCII art banner with version information during full startup of the CLI.
Renders a compact one-line banner for quick or sub-command invocations.
Implement promptForExplicitConfirmation: an async confirmation dialog that accepts a message and returns a boolean based on typed user input ('y'/'n'), using the 'prompts' library.
Implement promptForImmediateConfirmation: an async confirmation that waits for a single keypress ('y' or 'n') in raw mode without requiring Enter, processing stdin directly with readline.
Handle Ctrl+C during immediate confirmation by cleaning up stdin, writing a newline, and exiting the process with status 130.
Provide colored output (green 'Yes', red 'No') for immediate confirmation responses using kleur.
Atomic file write: writes content to a temporary file and renames to target path, preventing corruption from partial writes.
Automatic directory creation: creates parent directories if they do not exist before writing.
Unique temporary file naming: uses cryptographic randomness (crypto.randomBytes) to generate temp file names.
File copy operation: provides a method to copy a file from source to destination (inferred from fs.copyFileSync usage).
Cleanup of stale temporary files: possibly removes leftover temp files (fs.unlinkSync usage).
Install or update the pre-commit hook script with logic to discover the spine CLI via multiple fallback paths.
Backup any existing pre-commit hook before modifying it, using the FileSystemManager for safe file operations.
Remove the ArchSpine pre-commit hook cleanly when requested, leaving the hooks directory intact.
Provide idempotent installation: skip if the hook is already up-to-date, update if outdated, append if no managed block exists.
Manage file-based lock files under the .spine directory to prevent concurrent access conflicts.
Acquire locks with retry logic (MAX_LOCK_RETRIES) and token generation using randomUUID.
Release locks and enforce cleanup on graceful shutdown, process exit, and uncaught exceptions via process event handlers.
Serialize and deserialize lock payloads (token + timestamp) for persistence and inspection.
Provide parse/serialize utility functions for external consumers to read or write lock files.
Replace backslashes with forward slashes to enforce POSIX path separators.
Strip leading './' and '/' to ensure repo-relative logical paths.
Provide a single deterministic normalization function used across all database and file system index operations.
invariants: Footprints are deterministic: same input always produces the same hash.
Normalization (sorting, trimming, deduplication) ensures stability regardless of source data ordering.
All synchronized blocks are wrapped by start/end markers defined in templates.
Managed file content is inserted or removed using bounded block patterns (start line + content + end line).
Normalized filenames are validated against a set of allowed agent instruction filenames.
Removal operations require the caller to specify whether the managed file itself should be deleted after clearing content.
Block start markers (e.g., AGENT_BLOCK_START, ARCHSPINE_V2_BLOCK_START) are fixed strings that must not change to remain consistent with parsers and view renderers that match them.
Ignore line lists (SEARCH_IGNORE_LINES, SPINEIGNORE_RECOMMENDED_LINES, LOCAL_GITIGNORE_LINES) reflect the current set of ArchSpine-managed exclusions and are kept in sync across the repository.
The set of exported symbols must always be the union of the two submodules' public APIs.
No additional exports or imports should be added to preserve its role as a pure aggregator.
Brand colors are hardcoded as hex #ec9877 (orange) and #ebefbb (cream).
Version is read once from disk and cached indefinitely.
Banners are printed synchronously via console.log.
Both functions always return a Promise<boolean>.
Immediate confirmation uses stdin raw mode and ensures cleanup on resolution or interruption.
Ctrl+C during immediate confirmation terminates the process.
File writes are atomic: the target file is either completely updated or untouched if the process is interrupted.
All parent directories required for a file path are created before any write.
Temporary files are always cleaned up after a successful write.
The managed hook script must always be delimited by the ArchSpine block markers (`# >>> ArchSpine pre-commit >>>` and `# <<< ArchSpine pre-commit <<<`).
The `.git/hooks` directory must exist for installation to succeed; otherwise the operation returns a skip result.
At most one process can hold the lock for a given .spine directory at any moment.
Lock files are always cleaned up when a process releases the lock or exits.
A lock token is a UUID unique per acquisition attempt.
Output always uses forward slashes as separators.
Output never starts with './' or '/'.
The function is pure and deterministic: same input always produces same output.

Exported type (likely an enum or union) that defines strategies for resolving artifact content paths. Used by artifact resolution logic.
Markdown comment marker indicating the start of the ArchSpine agent instruction block.
Markdown comment marker indicating the end of the ArchSpine agent instruction block.
Pre-built full agent instruction block with start/end markers and instructions for AI agents interacting with the repository.
Markdown comment marker indicating the start of the V2 knowledge graph briefing block.
Markdown comment marker indicating the end of the V2 knowledge graph briefing block.

Markdown comment marker indicating the end of the MCP tools overview block.

Markdown comment marker indicating the end of the health snapshot block.

Pre-built health snapshot block with start/end markers.
File name constant for the custom .ignore file used by ArchSpine's search mechanism to exclude noisy directories.
File name constant for the .spineignore file that controls semantic scanning of the repository.
Array of lines for the .ignore file, excluding generated and low-signal paths from agent workspace search.
Complete newline-separated string content for the .ignore file built from SEARCH_IGNORE_LINES.
Comment marker line indicating the start of the ArchSpine-recommended section in .spineignore.
